issues: 1d timeframe mislabelled bearish despite structurally bullish price action (higher highs/lows, positive EMA slope +2.1) — the market report flagged this but downstream agents may have consumed the raw bearish tag uncritically
Fundamentals report was 27.8h stale at decision time (refreshed during run, but the cached version fed to earlier agents was 7.8h old with a different price basis of $68,027 vs ~$67,218)
Shorting into F&G=12 (Extreme Fear) is historically contrarian-wrong — the trader acknowledged past reflection warnings but the system lacks a hard gate on sentiment extremes
Weekend execution timing (Saturday AEST) with 40-60% thinner books increases slippage risk on a swing trade that may need to hold through CPI — risk judge flagged but didn't adjust stop width
Aggressive analyst pushed for 2% risk sizing which would have violated the 1% equity cap — good that risk judge caught and corrected, but the aggressive analyst's calibration needs review
improvements: Add a reconciliation step that cross-checks raw indicator values (ema_slope, higher_highs/lows) against the directional label before passing to downstream agents — the 1d bearish mislabel could have cascaded into a false consensus

Implement a sentiment-extreme gate: when F&G ≤ 15 or ≥ 85, require the trader to explicitly address contrarian risk with a higher evidence bar (e.g., 8/10 conviction minimum for trend-following into extremes)
Fund manager's approval was sound but thin on independent analysis — FM should add at least one novel observation rather than summarizing the chain; currently reads as a rubber-stamp with extra steps

Track funding rate flip velocity as a first-class indicator with historical calibration — bull cited -0.0042% as significant but bear correctly noted real squeezes need -0.03%+; the system should have a quantified threshold
summary: The system produced a well-reasoned SHORT at 7/10 confidence with proper risk sizing (1.53 BTC, 1% equity risk). The debate was genuinely adversarial and added value — the bull's squeeze thesis was credible but correctly overruled by weight of evidence (5/6 bearish, macro risk-off, structural CHOCH). Key concern: the 1d timeframe mislabel (bearish tag on bullish structure) inflated bearish consensus, and shorting into Extreme Fear (F&G=12) carries historical contrarian risk that the system acknowledged but didn't gate against.
